data.narrative:
Boom Supersonic, a Series B frontier-tech venture in the Supersonic & Hypersonic sector, is valued at $1.5B post-money following $600M+ in total capital raised. The comparable universe for this stage is sparse: two public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and two private peers (Hermeus, Venus Aerospace) exist in the sector, but none meet minimum statistical thresholds for a reliable peer-median valuation anchor.

Stage-progression analysis, the primary applicable method here, applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $1.5B last post-money valuation, reflecting typical Series B to next-stage step-ups. This yields $2.9B. A peer-median approach was not applied due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables (fewer than three datapoints meeting narrow filters); using an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable methodological noise given the niche nature of supersonic/hypersonic development.

The triangulated range is $2.4B to $3.3B, with a median of $2.9B. This range rests on a single applicable method, which materially constrains confidence. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 1.9x stage multiplier in an early-stage, capital-intensive, highly specialized sector where comparable funding patterns may differ; the lack of peer pricing data to validate or adjust this progression; and the binary regulatory and technical risks inherent to supersonic certification that traditional venture stage models may not capture. Additional datapoints from later-stage raises or peer fundings would substantially improve conviction.
